question.text: An option has a theta of -0.05 per day. If 10 trading days pass (all else equal), by how much will the option premium decline?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Premium decline = |Theta| x Days = 0.05 x 10 = Rs. 0.50. Theta measures daily time decay. **The Trap:** Rs. 0.05 uses only one day. Rs. 5.00 likely misplaces the decimal. Rs. 0.005 divides instead of multiplying. **Cross-Exam Context:** Time decay calculations are common in NISM Equity Derivatives module. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Theta decay accelerates as expiry approaches. The last 30 days see the most rapid time decay, especially for ATM options.
